title: Vipassana (Insight)
blurb: Observing experience clearly to see how mind and reality work.
lead: Vipassana means 'insight' or 'clear seeing'. It's a foundational Buddhist practice of observing sensations, thoughts, and feelings as they arise and pass.

blocks.text: Vipassana is the practice of observing your moment-to-moment experience — sensations, thoughts, emotions — with steady, non-reactive attention, to see clearly how they arise and pass.
It is often taught in silent retreats and has strongly influenced modern mindfulness. As a spiritual and contemplative tradition, its deeper claims rest on lived practice rather than clinical trials.

items: Establish a stable posture and a few minutes of breath awareness to settle.
Broaden attention to whatever is most prominent — a sensation, sound, thought, or emotion.
Observe it without grasping or pushing away, noticing its changing nature.
Return to the breath as a home base whenever you feel scattered.
Consider learning from an experienced teacher or a structured course.

blocks.text: Diving into intensive practice without guidance. A teacher or course helps navigate difficult material that can surface.

resources.title: Free intro courses & talks
resources.whyHere: Huge free archive of insight-meditation talks from respected teachers.
